claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're inquiring about.

Let me start with: **Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children you're asking about?
- Their names and ages?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child I'm responsible for. I have one child - Alex, who is 9 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Alex is 9 years old, so they're under 16, which is good.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

**Does Alex normally live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your residency status:

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Now I need to ask about your immigration status:

**Do you have the right to reside in the UK? For example, are you a British citizen, have settled status, or have another immigration status that gives you the right to reside here?**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Now I need to ask an important question about whether anyone else might be claiming for Alex:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** For example, Alex's other parent or another family member?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Just a couple more questions:

**Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?** 

(This would be relevant if Alex were 16-19 years old, but I want to confirm there are no special circumstances. At 9 years old, Alex would typically be in primary school, which counts as education.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is just in primary school - Year 5, nothing special.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just one final question to complete the assessment:

**Is Alex currently in hospital or in local authority care for an extended period?** Or are there any other special living arrangements I should know about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. Alex is just living at home with me, going to school normally.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:

✅ **Alex (9 years old)** - You are eligible because:
- Alex is under 16 years old
- Alex lives with you
- You are a British citizen living in the UK (Manchester)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is living at home and attending primary school normally

You meet all the requirements for Child Benefit. You can now apply for Child Benefit if you haven't already done so. The application process can be completed online or by post.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 9 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is living at home attending primary school with no special circumstances such as being in hospital or local authority care. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in Manchester with their 9-year-old child Alex. Alex lives with them, attends primary school normally, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 9 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, the claimant is a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK, no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and Alex is living at home attending primary school with no special circumstances such as being in hospital or local authority care. All eligibility criteria are met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a British citizen living in Manchester with their 9-year-old child Alex. Alex lives with them, attends primary school normally, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. All eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.
